skill.name: grill-with-docs
skill.description: Guides a rigorous planning interview that checks proposals against project language, code behavior, CONTEXT.md, and ADRs.
skill.summary: Stress-test plans against domain terminology and capture decisions in project documentation.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are markdown formatting, example file paths, and legitimate documentation guidance. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection attempts were found in the scanned files. The remaining risk is low because the skill instructs agents to inspect project files and update CONTEXT.md or ADR documents.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings occur in markdown code fences, inline file names, and documentation examples. No executable command invocation or user-controlled shell execution was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are markdown examples and inline filenames, not executable source code. The scanned files contain no scripts or runtime command APIs.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Findings Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations are template prose and domain documentation examples. No hashing function, encryption API, key handling, or cryptographic implementation appears at those lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain natural-language descriptions and markdown placeholders. There is no semantic evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or any other weak cryptographic algorithm being used.

low_findings.title: Legitimate Codebase Exploration and Documentation Writes
low_findings.description: The skill asks the agent to inspect the codebase when questions can be answered from files and to update CONTEXT.md or ADR files. This is expected behavior for the skill, but users should review documentation changes before accepting them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file inspection and documentation-update behavior is explicit in the instructions. It is legitimate for this documentation skill, but it still creates a bounded filesystem risk.

content.value_statement: Planning discussions often drift away from existing domain language and past decisions. This skill challenges assumptions and records resolved terms or decisions in CONTEXT.md and ADRs.
actual_capabilities: Interviews the user one question at a time about a technical plan.
Checks plan language against existing CONTEXT.md terminology.
Explores the codebase when file evidence can answer a question.
Captures resolved domain terms in CONTEXT.md during the session.
Suggests ADRs only for hard-to-reverse, surprising trade-offs.
Provides concise ADR and context documentation formats.
limitations: It does not implement the plan or make architecture decisions alone.
It depends on the user to confirm domain meaning and trade-offs.
It may create or update documentation files during the workflow.
It is not a substitute for security, performance, or compliance review.

use_cases.title: Review a Proposed Architecture
use_cases.description: Challenge a new design against current code, terminology, and previous decisions before implementation starts.

use_cases.title: Clarify Domain Language
use_cases.description: Resolve ambiguous terms and document preferred vocabulary for a product area or bounded context.

use_cases.title: Capture Important Trade-Offs
use_cases.description: Decide whether a technical choice deserves an ADR and record the reason in a concise format.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Plan Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a proposed change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use grill-with-docs to review this plan. Ask one question at a time and check our existing docs before suggesting new terminology.

prompt_templates.title: Resolve Domain Terms
prompt_templates.prompt: Use grill-with-docs to identify ambiguous terms in this proposal. Compare them with CONTEXT.md and propose canonical names.

prompt_templates.title: Decide on ADR Coverage
prompt_templates.scenario: Determine whether a decision should be recorded.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use grill-with-docs to test whether this decision needs an ADR. Evaluate reversibility, surprise, and real trade-offs.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Multi-Context Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review across several bounded contexts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use grill-with-docs to inspect CONTEXT-MAP.md, identify the relevant contexts, and challenge this cross-context design against existing language and relationships.

output_examples.input: A developer proposes adding partial cancellation to orders.
output: The skill asks whether cancellation means the whole order or individual line items.
It checks existing context language for Order, Fulfillment, and Invoice.
It records the resolved term only after the user confirms the domain meaning.

output_examples.input: A team wants to choose synchronous HTTP between billing and ordering.
output: The skill probes why events are not sufficient for the workflow.
It checks whether existing ADRs already describe integration rules.
It suggests an ADR only if the trade-off is meaningful and hard to reverse.

best_practices: Start with a concrete plan and let the skill question one decision at a time.
Review every proposed CONTEXT.md or ADR edit before accepting it.
Use existing project terms unless the session deliberately resolves a better term.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to generate broad architecture documents without a specific plan.
Do not accept terminology changes before confirming them with domain experts.
Do not create ADRs for obvious, reversible, or purely routine choices.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps teams stress-test technical plans and keep domain documentation aligned with decisions.

faq.question: Does it write code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on questions, terminology, CONTEXT.md updates, and ADR guidance.

faq.question: Can it read my repository?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill instructs the agent to inspect files when code or documentation can answer a question.

faq.question: When should I create an ADR?
faq.answer: Create one when a decision is hard to reverse, surprising without context, and based on a real trade-off.

faq.question: Does it support multi-context repositories?
faq.answer: Yes. It can use CONTEXT-MAP.md to find related contexts and their documentation locations.

faq.question: What should users review carefully?
faq.answer: Users should review file reads, CONTEXT.md edits, and ADR drafts before accepting changes.
